summary: Added a Chapter Questline stage strip inside the selected Path Map Chapter Archive record deck so each chapter reads as a compact spawn/proof/gate/task/unlock/next-move run map before the deeper dossier modules.

queries: game UI progress timeline milestones blockers quest log dashboard design pattern
progressive disclosure timeline dashboard milestones blockers UX pattern
quest log UI milestones blockers progress dashboard game design
pattern_notes: Progressive disclosure keeps complex dashboards scannable by putting the highest-signal state first and deeper records behind focused interaction.
Progress-step patterns give users a fast sense of where a process stands, especially when labels and icons clarify each step.
Quest logs work when they summarize active and completed states while preserving a route back into detailed records.

ui_contract: Render Chapter Questline inside the selected Chapter Archive record deck before Spoils and deeper modules.
Show six stable stages: Spawn, Proof, Gate, Tasks, Unlock, and Next.
Tone stages as unlocked, gated, advancing, clear, locked, or scouting from existing chapter counts.
Expose real source stages as data-path-event-focus controls for the existing Event Proof lens.
Keep empty stages visible as inert markers so new standalone repos understand the expected chapter progression.
Use only browser-local interaction and do not mutate the snapshot or contact agents.

red_green.red: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_questline.py -q failed before frontend wiring on missing function pathChapterQuestlineStages.

browser_green_route.evidence: Rendered one Chapter Questline with six stages, four clickable source stages, an animated progress meter, cache-busted CSS/JS, no horizontal overflow, and zero console errors.

event_focus_action.evidence: The Proof stage resolved to exactly one data-path-event-focus target, clicked successfully, marked the matching chapter record active, and updated the existing Event Proof lens with no console errors.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports rendered one Questline with six stages, four clickable source stages, a visible progress meter, no horizontal overflow, no bad rectangles, no clipped stage text, and no console errors.
